title: Can see all message filters / top or bottom of message filter window in V 142?
creator: MozzieBob
content:
I have a lot of message filters that I've created over the years to filter out SPAM (as well as other more positive purposes like sorting mail to specific folders). I'd like to clean out the ones that are obsolete.

If I choose Tools > Message Filters, I get a display window on the screen with some of my message filters shown. But the reason it's "some" is that the top and bottom of the window are well off the screen display. I can double click on a Filter and edit its details and I can delete a filter by pressing the delete key. But I can't scroll through the list. Any filters that are out of range are not accessible.

Is this a bug I need to report? Or am I missing something?

title: I had to reinstall Thunderbird and now cannot get into my email that was thru Brighthouse which is now Spectrum.
creator: TinkerSax
content:
My email was originally set up with Brighthouse which was acquired by Spectrum. My email address has a cfl.rr.com extension. Because of a hard disk problem, I had to reload Thunderbird. Spectrum is still maintaining the old Brighthouse accounts but support is extremely limited.

I can now get into my account in Spectrum's email and can send and receive email there. I had to use a password I set up for Spectrum to do that. But Thunderbird will not recognize my cfl.rr.com account. I can import that account into Outlook and Gmail, but not Thunderbird. It appears to me that Thunderbird is looking for a password that Outlook and Gmail don't require. If I had an old Thunderbird or cfl.rr.com password I have lost it.

I prefer Thunderbird, and I need to get my address book and history files back. I have just set up a new email account in Gmail and imported it into Thunderbird. Is there a way I can I recover my old address book and history files? I do have all the old Thunderbird files that I backed up before I had to reload Thunderbird.

content: We have asked this before, and we still are not able to resolve it at all. Several items: We have two email addresses used by Thunderbird, for many years. Charles and Penny Kindt
